text.title: security-dast Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for security-dast. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: DAST MUST run against staging or a dedicated test environment only. Production URLs MUST NOT appear as DAST targets in any CI/CD configuration.
rule.rationale: DAST is an active scanner — it sends malformed requests, probes endpoints, and may trigger destructive operations. Running against production can cause outages, trip rate-limiters, and violate terms of service or compliance mandates.

rule.statement: Define a scan scope file (.zap/rules.tsv or equivalent) that explicitly excludes third-party services, payment processors, email senders, and test-data-destructive endpoints.
rule.rationale: Out-of-scope scanning can trigger real-money transactions, send test emails to real users, or delete data — regardless of whether the environment is labelled "staging".

rule.statement: Update Nuclei templates on a schedule (weekly); pin to a specific template version in CI. Do not float to HEAD of the community template repo.
rule.rationale: New Nuclei templates may be noisy or incorrect before community review; floating HEAD can cause CI to suddenly flag many false positives after an upstream template update.
